Gold.data_anomaly: Gold price $3980.50 is outside expected range ($4000-$7000). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: U.S. stocks rallied on Tuesday, with the S&P 500 up 0.8% and the Nasdaq jumping 1.7%, driven by a blowout session for semiconductors. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index surged 4.2% after South Korea announced a $1.3 trillion investment plan to expand domestic chipmaking capacity. WTI crude held near $69.57 as Hormuz shipping traffic continued to recover from the weekend flare-up, while gold extended its historic quarterly slump, falling to $3,980. The 10-year Treasury yield edged up to 4.42% ahead of Friday's Non-Farm Payrolls report, which will be the next major test for the Fed's hawkish stance.
commodities_note: Gold at $3,980.50 — the data source flags a potential anomaly (outside the expected $4,000−$7,000 range), likely reflecting the metal's largest quarterly drop since 2013. WTI crude at $69.57 is largely unmoved, with the Hormuz risk premium now fully unwound. The U.S. dollar index was flat at 101.33.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks hold near overbought levels as the U11 upgrade to WATCH halts the cluster's recent caution slide. OCBC continues to outperform DBS on relative NIM resilience, but no name offers a workable R:R — all three are +4–7% above their 50-day averages with RSI in the low 60s. The US10Y at 4.42% keeps the dual-channel NIM compression intact.
singapore.key_development: UOB upgraded to WATCH as the extension dipped below 5%, but the cluster still lacks an actionable entry.

semiconductors.summary: The Semis cluster surged 4.2% on Tuesday, led by AMD (+7.5%) and INTC (+6%) after South Korea's $1.3T chip investment plan (per WSJ, July 1) boosted the entire supply chain. Every name in the cluster is extended, with AMD, INTC, MU, ASML, 000660.KS, and SNDK all hard-blocked above 20% extremes. NVDA is the sole name below its SMA50 and carries the best sizing R:R, but a tight invalidation keeps it in CAUTION.
semiconductors.key_development: Korea's $1.3T investment plan confirms the memory supply build-out thesis, but extreme extensions block all entries.

big_tech.summary: MSFT was assigned ACCUMULATE as layoffs improve the cost story and R:R reaches 2.2:1 — the only BigTech name offering a near-term entry. GOOG and AMZN remain CAUTION with tight resistance levels capping upside; the cluster remains divergent as AI capex concerns weigh on the mega-cap group while semis suppliers thrive.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT layoffs and R:R compression create the first ACCUMULATE signal in BigTech since the June selloff.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment partner — direct read on AI optical interconnect demand alongside watchlist name LITE.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's next earnings confirm >$500M in NVDA photonics purchase commitments.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC design wins (Amazon Trainium, Google Axion) make it a direct AVGO competitor — a read on hyperscaler ASIC spend trajectory.
interconnected.entry_note: Scout if AVGO or GOOG chip commentary indicates an acceleration of custom-silicon capex in Q3.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Largest memory maker by volume and a direct beneficiary of the $1.3T Korean chip investment plan — memory ASP cycle cross-read on MU and 000660.KS.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after Samsung's Q2 earnings for DRAM ASP guidance to confirm the memory up-cycle.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Leading data center switch vendor — a first-order beneficiary of AI networking buildouts, with direct linkage to NVDA Blackwell cluster deployments.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if NVDA's next earnings call quantifies Blackwell Ultra cluster-scale switch demand.

interconnected.name: KLA Corporation
interconnected.reason: Process control tools supplier directly in the slipstream of the $1.3T Korea fab buildout (per Simply Wall St., July 1) — links to ASML and TSM wafer capacity expansion.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch for KLAC to test its 52-week high on volume; add to watchlist if it breaks above $304 on above-average volume.

active_risks: US-Iran ceasefire remains fragile after weekend tit-for-tat strikes; Hormuz traffic recovering but sporadic attacks continue (per Axios, June 29).
US working on a ban targeting Chinese energy inverters (per Reuters, June 30), widening the technology decoupling front beyond semiconductors.
North American trade deal in limbo as Trump refuses to commit to USMCA ahead of the July 1 review deadline (per Politico, June 30).
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil risk premium fully unwound with WTI near pre-war levels, allowing semis to refocus on demand fundamentals. The energy inverter ban adds a new tariff front, but no watchlist ticker has direct exposure. The USMCA uncertainty is a background risk for AMNZ and the broader market, but no immediate price impact yet.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran says it is selling oil at a 20% premium after exporting 40 million barrels (per CNBC, July 1); US Commerce Department working on an inverter ban (per Reuters, June 30).

base.description: Ceasefire holds, Hormuz flows normalize fully; WTI trades $65–70. Semis remain extended but demand thesis drives gradual consolidation; NVDA and MSFT lead the next leg of entry opportunities.

optimistic.description: A permanent US-Iran deal is signed, lifting all sanctions; oil drops below $60, inflation fears recede, and the Fed signals a rate pause. Semis and BigTech both rally, opening entry windows across the watchlist.

pessimistic.description: Weekend strikes escalate into a new phase of conflict; Hormuz traffic is disrupted again, sending WTI above $90. Semis sell off on stagflation fears, and AI capex spending is questioned — all entries are put on hold.

wildcard.description: A US-China trade deal unexpectedly emerges from the soybean purchase momentum, removing the inverter ban and easing rare earth export controls. ASML and Chinese-exposed semis rally sharply, but the re-rating may be short-lived.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash; no new entries until either a conviction pullback in extended semis or a decisive break above SMA50 in NVDA/MSFT on volume.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 11 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, keeping the majority of the semis cluster out of reach. A fragile US-Iran ceasefire and hawkish Fed posture cap upside, but MSFT offers a disciplined entry.

scheduled_tech_events.notes: AI kit, on-device model integration, Vision Pro 2 rumours. Competitive signal for MSFT Copilot and GOOG Gemini on-device.

accumulate.note: Enter $370–375, invalidation at $355.51. R:R 2.2:1 to $412. Starter position — fragility: Azure/OpenAI credit circularity.

watch_for_entry.note: Upgraded to WATCH after extension dipped below +5%. Wait for consolidation to SGD 37.85 (SMA50) to improve R:R.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Consider entry near SGD 37.85, stop below SGD 37.42

watch_for_entry.note: Still +6.5% above SMA50; wait for pullback to SGD 61.60 for a better R:R.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Consider entry near SGD 61.60, stop below SGD 61.60

watch_for_entry.note: Similar to DBS — wait for pullback to SGD 23.32 (SMA50) for a clean entry.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Consider entry near SGD 23.32, stop below SGD 23.12

regime_change_pending.note: RCP consolidating (session 18/25) after Oracle Project Jupiter gap. No entry until graduation. Await Path A retest of post-gap SMA50 near $276 or Path B breakout above $304.80.

on_deck.note: Sizing R:R 2.3:1 to structural support $194.51 — on deck for upgrade on a reclaim of SMA50 at $209.83 with volume.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 0.59, forward PE 15.7 — cheapest name in semis with 85% revenue growth.

on_deck.note: R:R 2.1:1 and PEG 1.32 — on deck but held back by antitrust probe and below SMA50; upgrade on reclaim of $367.
on_deck.rationale: Forward PE 24.3 in line with cluster, 22% revenue growth.
on_deck.trigger: Reclaim of $367.13 (SMA50) on volume with no further negative antitrust developments.

on_deck.note: +29% extreme extension keeps it blocked, but PEG 1.25 and MI450 framework provide fundamental support.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 1.25, revenue growth 38% — fundamentals reasonable; technicals the blocker.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to $450 (SMA50) or consolidation that allows the 50-day to catch up.

on_deck.note: Hard-blocked at +26%; RCP failed. On deck only if new external 18A customer wins or consolidation to $111.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 1.36 but forward PE 90x and negative FCF — the foundry thesis is unproven.
on_deck.trigger: Consolidation to $111 (SMA50) or a confirmed, named external 18A customer.

on_deck.note: Earnings acceleration confirms thesis, but +39% extreme blocks entry. On deck for pullback to $854.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 0.17, forward PE 7.7 — the cycle is real; technicals are the only blocker.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to $854 (SMA50) or a 10%+ correction from current levels.

on_deck.note: CoWoS capacity raise keeps demand story hot, but +15% hard block. On deck for pullback to $416.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 1.36, forward PE 23.7 — fair value for a capacity-constrained monopoly.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to $415.91 (SMA50) or consolidation to allow the 50-day to catch up.

on_deck.note: NAND cycle strong, but +45% extreme extension — on deck for a pullback to $1,571 or shorter-term support at $1,514.
on_deck.rationale: Forward PE 12.2 (cyclical peak) — cheap on the surface but not a structural entry.

on_deck.note: Custom ASIC thesis intact, but R:R 0.7:1 and below SMA50 — on deck for SMA50 reclaim at $410.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 0.66, forward PE 19.5 — attractive valuation for a blended hardware/software model.

on_deck.note: NVDA photonics partnership and S&P 500 inclusion are tailwinds, but sizing R:R only 1.2:1 — on deck for pullback to $803.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 0.63, revenue growth 90% — optics demand is real, but the stock must settle.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to $803.59 (structural support) or consolidation above SMA50.

on_deck.note: AWS partnership expands networking optics — on deck but sizing R:R 0.6:1. Upgrade on SMA50 reclaim.
on_deck.rationale: Forward PE 27.3, FCF yield 2.15% — value is reasonable for a slow compounder.

on_deck.note: ARIA launch is a step forward, but negative FCF and below SMA50 keep it on deck; upgrade on $110 reclaim.
on_deck.rationale: Revenue growth 112% but no earnings — speculative neocloud model.

on_deck.note: Near oversold with R:R 1.5:1 — on deck for a reclaim of $6.68 to confirm bottom.
on_deck.rationale: Revenue growth +58% YoY, but ADR delisting risk caps upside.
on_deck.trigger: Reclaim of $6.68 resistance on above-average volume.

on_deck.note: Oversold with R:R 4.5:1 — on deck for stabilization near $17.50 support.
on_deck.rationale: GaN power chip leader in a secular growth market.
on_deck.trigger: Hold above $17.50 support with a volume spike; then re-evaluate R:R.

on_deck.note: SiC datacenter pivot thesis intact, but +15% hard block; on deck for pullback to €70.88.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 0.74, forward PE 30.8 — reasonable for a power-semi leader.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to €70.88 (SMA50) or a named hyperscaler SiC contract win.

avoid.note: [Rebucketed CAUTION → avoid (PEG 2.69 >= 1.5; FCF yield 1.08% <= 2%)] +22% extreme extension, priced for perfection into July 15 earnings. No entry pre-print.
[Rebucketed CAUTION → avoid (fwd PE 45.4x >= 20x (no PEG); FCF yield 0.42% <= 2%)] RCP gap fill invalidated the photonics catalyst; no entry until a new confirmed deal.
[Rebucketed CAUTION → avoid (PEG 3.60 >= 1.5; FCF yield 1.41% <= 2%)] +29% extreme extension. The thesis is intact but the price is wrong.
[Rebucketed CAUTION → avoid (fwd PE 92.6x >= 20x (no PEG); FCF yield -0.54% <= 2%)] China permit risk gates InP shipments; 93x PE is unjustifiable without clarity.
[Rebucketed CAUTION → avoid (fwd PE 60.0x >= 20x (no PEG); FCF yield 0.51% <= 2%)] SmartSiC write-downs and -35% revenue decline swamp Photonics-SOI optionality.
[Rebucketed CAUTION → avoid (PEG 1.83 >= 1.5; FCF yield 0.38% <= 2%)] stuck below SMA50 with no catalyst until July 31 earnings. Tight resistance at $238.91 caps upside.
[Rebucketed CAUTION → avoid (PEG 1.62 >= 1.5; FCF yield 0.63% <= 2%)] 56x forward PE for a defense AI name with no near-term earnings catalyst.
[Rebucketed CAUTION → avoid (PEG 3.82 >= 1.5; FCF yield 0.04% <= 2%)] +9.3% extension, 69x PE. MEMS timing thesis is real but the entry is not.

hold_no_action.note: Revenue growth solid but margin concerns persist. Reassess after Aug 12 earnings.
MOCVD monopoly intact, R:R 1.6:1 improving. Wait for volume confirmation and €55.28 breakout.

new_stocks_to_watch.name: Vertiv Holdings
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_note: Directly linked to AI data-center power and cooling infrastructure — add to watchlist if BE's Brookfield deal signals a broader infrastructure investment wave.

new_stocks_to_watch.name: Cadence Design Systems
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_note: Beneficiary of chip design complexity as INTC's 14A and NVDA's Vera Rubin ramp — scout if INTC's 14A wins additional external customers.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: NFP <150k, unemployment rises → rate hike odds ease, yields dip, relief rally in technology
bullish_upgrades: NVDA → WATCH (rate relief eases SMA50 drag)
GOOG → WATCH (momentum shifts on macro tailwind)
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: NFP >200k, wage growth accelerates → Fed hawkishness intensifies, semis sell off
bearish_impact: NVDA → deeper CAUTION (rate risk compounds below SMA50)
AMZN → deeper CAUTION (no catalyst into earnings)

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=3) / ACCUMULATE (n=4) spanning trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
